01 playground

  • week 1 examples
    • lifesat ~ treatment + day + (1 + day | ppt)
      • simple lm
      • lmm
      • grouping structure of day7 data
      • grouping structure of week data
    • attentiveness ~ hour + (1 + hour | child)
    • correct ~ trial_type + (1 + trial_type | ppt) + (1 | item)
  • week 1 lab examples
    • y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts)
    • y ~ x
    • y ~ x
    • y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts) + (1 | itms)
    • y ~ x + (1 | itms)
    • y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts) + (1 + x | itms)
    • y ~ x + z + (1 + x + z | ppts) + (1 + x | itms)
    • y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts) + (1 | itms)
    • y ~ x + (1 | itms)
    • y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts)
library(tidyverse)
library(simglm)

week 1 examples

lifesat ~ treatment + day + (1 + day | ppt)

lifesat: continuous, measured after practicing each method daily for a week treatment: journal, meditation (btwn) day

half_ppts <- 18
journal <- sim_int_1grp_1pred(seed=2, b0 = 1, b1=.1, N = half_ppts*7, n_groups = half_ppts) |>
  mutate(
    condition = 'journal',
    ppt_id = paste0('j', g)
  ) |>
  select(-y_bin, -g) 
meditate <- sim_int_1grp_1pred(seed=4, b0 = 1, b1=.5, N = half_ppts*7, n_groups = half_ppts) |>
  mutate(
    condition = 'meditate',
    ppt_id = paste0('m', g)
  ) |>
  select(-y_bin, -g)

lifesat_dat <- bind_rows(journal, meditate) |>
  rename(day = x1) |>
  mutate(lifesat = datawizard::rescale(y, to = c(15, 75))) |>
  select(-y)

simple lm

btwn ppt: day 7 only

lifesat_dat_7 <- lifesat_dat |>
  filter(day == 7) |>
  select(-day)

lifesat_dat_7 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = lifesat)) +
  geom_violin()

what categ vars in dataset?

lifesat_dat_7
  • condition
  • ppt_id

what categ vars not an outcome or a predictor?

  • ppt_id

what levels?

lifesat_dat_7$ppt_id
 [1] "j1"  "j2"  "j3"  "j4"  "j5"  "j6"  "j7"  "j8"  "j9"  "j10" "j11" "j12" "j13" "j14" "j15" "j16"
[17] "j17" "j18" "m1"  "m2"  "m3"  "m4"  "m5"  "m6"  "m7"  "m8"  "m9"  "m10" "m11" "m12" "m13" "m14"
[33] "m15" "m16" "m17" "m18"

is each level associated with more than one data point?

lifesat_dat_7 |>
  group_by(ppt_id) |>
  count()

nope, appears only 1x.

lmm

Good for Week 1 and Week 2

lifesat_dat |>
  ggplot(aes(x = day, y = lifesat, colour = condition)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ ppt_id)

what categ vars in dataset?

  • condition
  • ppt_id

what categ vars not an outcome or a predictor?

  • ppt_id

what levels?

lifesat_dat$ppt_id
  [1] "j1"  "j1"  "j1"  "j1"  "j1"  "j1"  "j1"  "j2"  "j2"  "j2"  "j2"  "j2"  "j2"  "j2"  "j3"  "j3" 
 [17] "j3"  "j3"  "j3"  "j3"  "j3"  "j4"  "j4"  "j4"  "j4"  "j4"  "j4"  "j4"  "j5"  "j5"  "j5"  "j5" 
 [33] "j5"  "j5"  "j5"  "j6"  "j6"  "j6"  "j6"  "j6"  "j6"  "j6"  "j7"  "j7"  "j7"  "j7"  "j7"  "j7" 
 [49] "j7"  "j8"  "j8"  "j8"  "j8"  "j8"  "j8"  "j8"  "j9"  "j9"  "j9"  "j9"  "j9"  "j9"  "j9"  "j10"
 [65] "j10" "j10" "j10" "j10" "j10" "j10" "j11" "j11" "j11" "j11" "j11" "j11" "j11" "j12" "j12" "j12"
 [81] "j12" "j12" "j12" "j12" "j13" "j13" "j13" "j13" "j13" "j13" "j13" "j14" "j14" "j14" "j14" "j14"
 [97] "j14" "j14" "j15" "j15" "j15" "j15" "j15" "j15" "j15" "j16" "j16" "j16" "j16" "j16" "j16" "j16"
[113] "j17" "j17" "j17" "j17" "j17" "j17" "j17" "j18" "j18" "j18" "j18" "j18" "j18" "j18" "m1"  "m1" 
[129] "m1"  "m1"  "m1"  "m1"  "m1"  "m2"  "m2"  "m2"  "m2"  "m2"  "m2"  "m2"  "m3"  "m3"  "m3"  "m3" 
[145] "m3"  "m3"  "m3"  "m4"  "m4"  "m4"  "m4"  "m4"  "m4"  "m4"  "m5"  "m5"  "m5"  "m5"  "m5"  "m5" 
[161] "m5"  "m6"  "m6"  "m6"  "m6"  "m6"  "m6"  "m6"  "m7"  "m7"  "m7"  "m7"  "m7"  "m7"  "m7"  "m8" 
[177] "m8"  "m8"  "m8"  "m8"  "m8"  "m8"  "m9"  "m9"  "m9"  "m9"  "m9"  "m9"  "m9"  "m10" "m10" "m10"
[193] "m10" "m10" "m10" "m10" "m11" "m11" "m11" "m11" "m11" "m11" "m11" "m12" "m12" "m12" "m12" "m12"
[209] "m12" "m12" "m13" "m13" "m13" "m13" "m13" "m13" "m13" "m14" "m14" "m14" "m14" "m14" "m14" "m14"
[225] "m15" "m15" "m15" "m15" "m15" "m15" "m15" "m16" "m16" "m16" "m16" "m16" "m16" "m16" "m17" "m17"
[241] "m17" "m17" "m17" "m17" "m17" "m18" "m18" "m18" "m18" "m18" "m18" "m18"

is each level associated with more than one data point?

lifesat_dat

one way: just look at the df. the answer is yes bc j1 appears more than 1x, j2 appears more than 1x, etc.

grouping structure of day7 data

grouping structure of week data

another way, more efficient: use tidyverse to count how many times each ppt label appears.

lifesat_dat |>
  group_by(ppt_id) |>
  count()

attentiveness ~ hour + (1 + hour | child)

Good for Week 1 and Week 2

children’s attentiveness at school across the day

attent <- sim_int_1grp_1pred(seed=1, b0 = 2, b1=-.2, N = 140) |>
  rename(
    hour = x1,
    child = g,
    attentiveness = y
  ) |>
  select(-y_bin) |>
  mutate(
    hour = hour - 1,
    child = factor(child),
    attentiveness_z = attentiveness - mean(attentiveness)
  )
 
attent |>
  ggplot(aes(x = hour, y = attentiveness_z)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ child, labeller = label_both)

What categorical variables does your data contain?

  • child

How many times does each each level of this variable appear in the data?

attent |>
  group_by(child) |>
  count()

each child contributes seven data points to the data set. so the data has a grouping by child.

correct ~ trial_type + (1 + trial_type | ppt) + (1 | item)

Good for Week 1 and Week 2

How does people’s accuracy on an experimental task differ between conditions, and how is this difference affected by people’s DASS scores?

dass <- read_csv('../data/dass.csv')
dass |> head(20)

what categ vars in dataset?

  • ppt_id
  • target_id
  • condition
  • correct

what categ vars not an outcome or a predictor?

  • ppt_id
  • target_id

for each categ var, what levels does it have?

dass$ppt_id |> unique()
dass$target_id |> unique()

is each of those levels associated with more than one data point?

dass |>
  group_by(ppt_id) |>
  count()

yes

dass |>
  group_by(target_id) |>
  count()

yes.

week 1 lab examples

y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts)

d1 <- simcross(seed=1, b3 = 3, n_itms = 6, n_ppts = 12, n_obs_per = 3) |>
  select(ppts, y, x) |>
  mutate(y = datawizard::rescale(y, to = c(0, 12)))

d1 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(x), y = y, colour = factor(ppts))) +
  facet_wrap(~ppts) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')

y ~ x

d2 <- simcross(seed=2, b3 = 3, n_itms = 2, n_ppts = 4, n_obs_per = 24) |>
  select(y, x) |>
  rowid_to_column() |>
  rename(ppt_id = rowid) |>
  mutate(
    y = datawizard::rescale(y, to = c(0, 48)),
  )

d2

y ~ x

d3 <- MASS::mvrnorm(64, mu = c(0, 0), Sigma = matrix(c(5, 1, 1, 7), nrow=2, byrow=T)) |>
  as_tibble() |>
  rowid_to_column() |>
  rename(
    y = V1,
    x = V2,
    book = rowid
  )

d3 

d3 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y)) + geom_point()

y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts) + (1 | itms)

d4 <- simcross(seed=4, b0 = 20, b2 = 10, n_itms = 20) |>
  select(ppts, itms, y, wi_ppts_btw_itms) |>
  rename(x = wi_ppts_btw_itms)

d4 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(x), y = y, colour = factor(itms))) +
  facet_wrap(~ppts) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')
d4 |>
  group_by(ppts) |>
  count()

d4 |>
  group_by(itms) |>
  count()

^ grouping structures for both ppts and items

d4 |>
  group_by(ppts, x) |>
  count()
  • each ppt sees both vals of x, so rd slope is poss
d4 |>
  group_by(itms, x) |>
  count()
  • each item does NOT appear w both vals of x, so rd slope is not poss

y ~ x + (1 | itms)

d5 <- simcross(seed=5, b0 = -10, b2 = -5) |>
  select(itms, y, wi_ppts_btw_itms) |>
  rename(x = wi_ppts_btw_itms)

d5 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(x), y = y, colour = factor(itms))) +
  geom_point()
  • by-item grouping struc bc rep’d obs of ea item
  • but no slopes possible by item over cond x, bc not every item appears in every cond x
d5 |>
  group_by(itms, x) |>
  count()

y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts) + (1 + x | itms)

d6 <- simcross(seed=6, b0 = 4, b3 = 2, n_itms = 6, n_obs_per = 10) |>
  select(ppts, itms, y, x) |>
  mutate(
    # x = datawizard::rescale(x, to = c(pi, pi*3)),
    y = abs(log(y))
  )
  # rename(cond = wi_ppts_btw_itms)

d6 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y, colour = factor(itms))) +
  facet_wrap(~ ppts) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')

y ~ x + z + (1 + x + z | ppts) + (1 + x | itms)

d7 <- simcross(seed=7, b0 = 0, b3 = 0, n_itms = 2, n_obs_per = 6) |>
  select(ppts, itms, y, x, wi_ppts_btw_itms) |>
  rename(z = wi_ppts_btw_itms)

d7
d7 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(z), y = y, colour = factor(itms))) +
  facet_wrap(~ ppts, labeller = label_both) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')

y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts) + (1 | itms)

d8 <- simcross(seed=8, b0 = 0, b2 = -10, n_ppts = 4, n_itms = 8) |>
  select(ppts, itms, y, wi_ppts_btw_itms) |>
  rename(x = wi_ppts_btw_itms)

d8 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(x), y = y, colour = factor(itms))) +
  facet_wrap(~ppts) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')

y ~ x + (1 | itms)

d9 <- simcross(seed=9, b0 = -10, b2 = -5, n_itms = 50, n_obs_per = 5) |>
  select(itms, y, x) |>
  mutate(
    x = sample(x),
    y = abs(y)
    )

d9
d9 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y, colour = factor(itms))) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')

y ~ x + (1 + x | ppts)

d10 <- simcross(seed=10, b3 = 2, n_itms = 2, n_ppts = 6, n_obs_per = 8) |>
  select(ppts, y, wi_itms_wi_ppts) |>
  rename(x = wi_itms_wi_ppts)

d10 |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(x), y = y, colour = factor(ppts))) +
  facet_wrap(~ppts) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')
